message:
feat(core/insert-select): streaming live-vtab fast path + drop redundant flush_retained_autocommit_txn in btree tests

Two unrelated connection.rs adjustments grouped because they touch
nearby code and collide as a single dirty file.

## Streaming live-vtab insert fast path

The live-vtab insert path previously always collected the full
`LiveVtabInsertRow` set into memory via
`collect_live_vtab_insert_rows` before dispatching the insert.
That works fine for small inserts but loses the streaming-insert
benefit added in the previous commit when the source is a plain
`INSERT ... SELECT` with no triggers.

Add `try_execute_streaming_live_vtab_insert_select` as the live-vtab
analogue of the base streaming fallback. Run it only when ALL of
the following hold:

    - target is a live vtab (`is_live_vtab`)
    - no BEFORE INSERT or AFTER INSERT trigger is installed
    - the source is an `InsertSource::Select(...)` (not a VALUES
      list, which has nothing to stream)

If the helper returns `Some(affected_rows)` the path records the
statement changes and returns early; if it returns `None` the code
falls back to the existing `collect_live_vtab_insert_rows` path,
preserving the no-regression guarantee for rows/VALUES inserts and
inserts on tables with triggers installed.

Also introduces a small supporting struct `LiveVtabInsertLayout`
(`default_sqls: Vec<Option<String>>`, `source_targets: Vec<Option<usize>>`)
that captures the per-column default-SQL expressions and the
source→target column mapping needed by the streaming live-vtab
path. The collect path already computed this information inline;
lifting it into a named struct lets the streaming helper reuse the
same layout without re-deriving the mapping per row.

## Drop redundant flush_retained_autocommit_txn in btree test helpers

Four test helpers in `mod tests` (`read_root_count` in two cases,
`read_root_count` with `is_table`, the leaf-page inspector, and
`read_index_entries`) used to call

    if conn.retained_autocommit_txn.borrow().is_some() {
        conn.flush_retained_autocommit_txn(&cx).unwrap();
    }

before running `invalidate_cached_write_txn` / `invalidate_cached_read_snapshot`
and acquiring a fresh read txn. The invalidate calls already
implicitly drain the retained autocommit txn through the same code
path, so the explicit flush was a no-op safety net that dated from
a pre-invalidation refactor. Remove the four occurrences so the
test helpers state their intent cleanly.
